\(\dfrac{1}{a}+\dfrac{1}{b}=\)
A. \(\dfrac{a+b}{ab\)}\).
B. \(\dfrac{ab}{a+b}\).
C. \(\dfrac{1}{ab}\).
D. \(\dfrac{2}{a+b}\).
E. \(\dfrac{1}{a+b}\).

For what value(s) of \(x\) does the equality \(\dfrac{(x+1)(x-2)}{x-2}=x+1\) hold?
A. \(-1\) only
B. \(2\) only
C. Any value
D. Any value except \(-1\).
E. Any value except \(2\).

Which of the following is a factor of \(4(a+b)^2-9(a-b)^2\)?
A. \(5b-a\)
B. \(5a+b\)
C. \(-a-b\)
D. \(13b-5a\)
E. \(13a-5b\)

If \(\dfrac{a}{b}=\dfrac{c}{d}=k\) and \(a, b, c, d\) are positive, then which of the following \textit{must} be true?
A. \(\dfrac{a+c}{b+d}=k\)
B. \(ab=cd=k\)
C. \(ac=bd=k\)
D. \(a=c=k\)
E. \(\dfrac{ac}{bd}=k\)

Simplify \(\dfrac{\overbrace{n\times n\times \dots \times n}^{n\text{ times}}}{\underbrace{n+n+\dots+n}_{n\text{ terms}}}\).
A. \(n^{n-2}\)
B. \(n^{\frac{n}{2}}\)
C. \(n-2\)
D. \(\dfrac{n}{2}\)
E. \(1\)

If \(a\) and \(b\) are greater than 1, which of the following statements is/are true?
I. \(\sqrt{a+b}=\sqrt{a}+\sqrt{b}\)
II. \((a^{-1}+b^{-1})^{-1}=a+b\)
III. \(a^2b^3=(ab)^6\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. None of them

Suppose \(x\) varies directly as \(y^2\) and inversely as \(z\). Find the percentage increase of \(x\) when \(y\) is increased by 20% and \(z\) is decreased by 20%.
A. 15.2%
B. 20%
C. 50%
D. 72.8%
E. 80%

A sum of \$10\,000 is deposited at 4% p.a., compounded yearly. Find the interest earned in the \textit{second year}.
A. \$16
B. \$400
C. \$416
D. \$800
E. \$816

The figure shows a solid platform with steps on one side and a slope on the other. Find its volume.

A. \(0.75\text{ m}^3\)
B. \(0.84\text{ m}^3\)
C. \(0.858\text{ m}^3\)
D. \(1.008\text{ m}^3\)
E. \(1.608\text{ m}^3\)

In the figure, \(TP\) and \(TQ\) are tangent to the circle of radius 3\text{ cm}. Find the length of the minor arc \(PQ\).

A. \(3\pi\text{ cm}\)
B. \(2\pi\text{ cm}\)
C. \(\dfrac{3\pi}{2}\text{ cm}\)
D. \(\pi\text{ cm}\)
E. \(\dfrac{\pi}{2}\text{ cm}\)

Find the ratio of the volume of the tetrahedron \(ACHD\) to the volume of the cube \(ABCDEFGH\) in the figure.

A. \(1:8\)
B. \(1:6\)
C. \(1:4\)
D. \(1:3\)
E. \(1:2\)

In the figure, the equilateral \(\Delta ACE\) of side 4\text{ cm} is inscribed in the circle. Find the area of the inscribed regular hexagon \(ABCDEF\).

A. \(8\sqrt{3}\text{ cm}^2\)
B. \(8\sqrt{2}\text{ cm}^2\)
C. \(4\sqrt{3}\text{ cm}^2\)
D. \(4\sqrt{2}\text{ cm}^2\)
E. \(16\text{ cm}^2\)

In the figure, a cone of height \(3h\) is cut by a plane parallel to its base into a smaller cone of height \(h\) and a frustum. Find the ratio of the volume of the smaller cone to the volume of the frustum.

A. \(1:27\)
B. \(1:26\)
C. \(1:9\)
D. \(1:8\)
E. \(1:7\)

The greatest value of \(1-2\sin\theta\) is
A. \(5\).
B. \(3\).
C. \(1\).
D. \(0\).
E. \(-1\).

In the figure, find \(\cos\theta\).

A. \(-\dfrac{1}{4}\)
B. \(-\dfrac{1}{3}\)
C. \(-\dfrac{1}{2}\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\)
E. \(\dfrac{1}{4}\)

In which two quadrants will the solution(s) of \(\sin\theta\cos\theta \lt 0\) lie?
A. In quadrants I and II only.
B. In quadrants I and III only.
C. In quadrants II and III only.
D. In quadrants II and IV only.
E. In quadrants III and IV only.

If \(A+B+C=180^\circ\), then \(1+\cos A\cos(B+C)=\)
A. \(0\).
B. \(\sin^2A\).
C. \(1+\cos^2A\).
D. \(1+\sin A\cos A\).
E. \(1-\sin A\cos A\).

The figure shows the graph of the function

A. \(\tan(x+\pi)\).
B. \(\tan(x-\pi)\).
C. \(\pi\tan x\).
D. \(\pi+\tan x\).
E. \(\pi-\tan x\).

Which of the following equations has/have solutions?
I. \(2\cos^2\theta-\sin^2\theta=1\)
II. \(2\cos^2\theta-\sin^2\theta=2\)
III. \(2\cos^2\theta-\sin^2\theta=3\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

In the figure, \(O\) is the center of the circle, find \(\theta\).

A. \(42^\circ\)
B. \(36^\circ\)
C. \(24^\circ\)
D. \(21^\circ\)
E. \(18^\circ\)

In the figure, \(ABC\) is a square. If \(BE=CE=5\), find \(AE\).

A. \(\sqrt{61}\)
B. \(9\)
C. \(10\)
D. \(6\sqrt{3}\)
E. \(\sqrt{109}\)

In the figure, the circle is inscribed in a regular pentagon. \(P\), \(Q\) and \(R\) are points of contact. Find \(\theta\).

A. \(30^\circ\)
B. \(32^\circ\)
C. \(35^\circ\)
D. \(36^\circ\)
E. \(45^\circ\)

In the figure, \(ST\) is a tangent to the smaller circle. \(ABC\) is a straight line. If \(\angle TAD=2x\) and \(\angle DPC=3x\), find \(x\).

A. \(30^\circ\)
B. \(36^\circ\)
C. \(40^\circ\)
D. \(42^\circ\)
E. \(45^\circ\)

If the two lines \(2x-y+1=0\) and \(ax+3y-1=0\) do not intersect, then \(a=\)
A. \(-6\).
B. \(-2\).
C. \(2\).
D. \(3\).
E. \(6\).

If \(0 \lt k \lt h\), which of the following circles intersect(s) the \(y\)-axis?
I. \((x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=k^2\)
II. \((x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=h^2\)
III. \((x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=h^2+k^2\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

If the line \(y=mx+3\) divides the circle \(x^2+y^2-4x-2y-5=0\) into two equal parts, find \(m\).
A. \(-\dfrac{1}{4}\)
B. \(-1\)
C. \(0\)
D. \(\dfrac{5}{4}\)
E. \(2\)

The mid-points of the sides of a triangle are \((3,4)\), \((2,0)\) and \((4,2)\). Which of the following points is a vertex of the triangle?
A. \((3,5)\)
B. \((3,2)\)
C. \((3,1)\)
D. \((1.5,2)\)
E. \((1,2)\)

The table shows the mean marks of two classes of students in a mathematics test
Class A: 38 students, Mean 72. Class B: 42 students, Mean 54. A student in Class A has scored 91 marks. It is found that his score was wrongly recorded as 19 in the calculation of the mean mark for Class A in the above table. Find the correct mean mark of the 80 students in the two classes
A. 61.65.
B. 62.55.
C. 63.
D. 63.45.
E. 63.9.

Two cards are drawn randomly from five cards \(A, B, C, D\) and \(E\). Find the probability that card \(A\) is drawn while card \(C\) is not.
A. \(\dfrac{3}{25}\)
B. \(\dfrac{3}{20}\)
C. \(\dfrac{4}{25}\)
D. \(\dfrac{6}{25}\)
E. \(\dfrac{3}{10}\)
The figure shows the cumulative frequency curves of three distributions. Arrange the three distributions in the order of their standard deviation.

If the quadratic equation \(ax^2-2bx+c=0\) has two equal roots, which of the following is/are true?
I. \(a, b, c\) form an arithmetic sequence.
II. \(a, b, c\) form a geometric sequence.
III. Both roots are \(\dfrac{b}{a}\).
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

Which of the following intervals \textit{must} contain a root of \(2x^3-x^2-x-3=0\)?
I. \(-1 \lt x \lt 1\) II. \(0 \lt x \lt 2\) III. \(1 \lt x \lt 3\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

How many integers \(x\) satisfy inequality \(6x^2-7x-20 \leq 0\)?
A. 0
B. 1
C. 2
D. 3
E. 4

From the figure, if \(\alpha \leq x \leq \beta\), then

A. \(ax^2+(b-m)x+(c-k) \leq 0\).
B. \(ax^2+(b-m)x+(c-k) \lt 0\).
C. \(ax^2+(b-m)x+(c-k) = 0\).
D. \(ax^2+(b-m)x+(c-k) \gt 0\).
E. \(ax^2+(b-m)x+(c-k) \geq 0\).

Under which of the following conditions \textit{must} the mean of \(n\) consecutive positive integers also be an integer?
A. \(n\) is any positive integer
B. \(n\) is any positive odd integer
C. \(n\) is any positive even integer
D. \(n\) is any multiple of 3
E. \(n\) is the square of any positive integer

If a polynomial \(f(x)\) is divisible by \(x-1\), then \(f(x-1)\) is divisible by
A. \(x-2\).
B. \(x+2\).
C. \(x-1\).
D. \(x+1\).
E. \(x\).

If the price of an orange rises by \$1, then 5 fewer oranges could be bought for \$100. Which of the following equations gives the original price \(x\) of an orange?
A. \(\dfrac{100}{x+1}=5\)
B. \(\dfrac{100}{x+1}-\dfrac{100}{x}=5\)
C. \(\dfrac{100}{x}-\dfrac{100}{x+1}=5\)
D. \(\dfrac{100}{x-1}-\dfrac{100}{x}=5\)
E. \(\dfrac{100}{x}-\dfrac{100}{x-1}=5\)

By selling an article at 10% discount off the marked price, a shop still makes 20% profit. If the cost price of the article is \$19\,800, then the marked price is
A. \$21\,600.
B. \$26\,136.
C. \$26\,400.
D. \$27\,225.
E. \$27\,500.

Coffee A and coffee B are mixed in the ratio \(x:y\) by weight. A costs \$50/kg and B costs \$40/kg. If the cost of A is increased by 10% while that of B is decreased by 15%, the cost of the mixture per kg remains unchanged. Find \(x:y\).
A. \(2:3\)
B. \(5:6\)
C. \(6:5\)
D. \(3:2\)
E. \(55:34\)

B. \(\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{8}}\)
C. \(\dfrac{3}{8}\)
D. \(\sqrt{\dfrac{2}{7}}\)
E. \(\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\)

C. \(\sin\dfrac{\theta}{2}=\dfrac{1}{3}\).

E. \(\sin\theta=\dfrac{3}{4}\).

B. \(25^\circ\)

A. I only

D. 8
